segments.text: For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day and sent them into his vineyard.
About nine in the morning he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
He told them,
You also go and work in my vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right.
segments.speaker: Landowner

segments.text: So they went. He went out again about noon and about three in the afternoon and did the same thing.
About five in the afternoon he went out and found still others standing around. He asked them,
Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?
segments.speaker: Landowner

segments.text: Because no one has hired us,
segments.speaker: Workers

segments.text: they answered. He said to them,
You also go and work in my vineyard.
segments.speaker: Landowner

segments.text: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman,
Call the workers and pay them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and going on to the first.
segments.speaker: Landowner

segments.text: The workers who were hired about five in the afternoon came and each received a denarius.
So when those came who were hired first, they expected to receive more. But each one of them also received a denarius.
When they received it, they began to grumble against the landowner.
These who were hired last worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the work and the heat of the day.
segments.speaker: First Workers

segments.text: But he answered one of them,
I am not being unfair to you, friend. Didn't you agree to work for a denarius?
segments.speaker: Landowner

segments.text: Take your pay and go. I want to give the one who was hired last the same as I gave you.
segments.speaker: Landowner

segments.text: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Or are you envious because I am generous?
segments.speaker: Landowner

segments.text: So the last will be first, and the first will be last.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: Jesus Predicts His Death a Third Time

segments.text: Now Jesus was going up to Jerusalem. On the way, he took the Twelve aside and said to them,
We are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and the teachers of the law. They will condemn him to death
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and will hand him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified. On the third day he will be raised to life!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the mother of Zebedee's sons came to Jesus with her sons and, kneeling down, asked a favor of him.
What is it you want? he asked.
Grant that one of these two sons of mine may sit at your right and the other at your left in your kingdom.
segments.speaker: Mother of Zebedee's Sons

segments.text: You don't know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup I am going to drink?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: We can,
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: You will indeed drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left is not for me to grant. These places belong to those for whom they have been prepared by my Father.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the ten heard about this, they were indignant with the two brothers.
Jesus called them together and said,
You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their high officials exercise authority over them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Not so with you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and whoever wants to be first must be your slave—
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As Jesus and his disciples were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
Two blind men were sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was going by, they shouted,
Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!
segments.speaker: Two Blind Men

segments.text: The crowd rebuked them and told them to be quiet, but they shouted all the louder,
Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!
segments.speaker: Two Blind Men

segments.text: Jesus stopped and called them.
What do you want me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, we want our sight.
segments.speaker: Two Blind Men

segments.text: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they received their sight and followed him.
